D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

The Lensmaker's Equation

reshape the two glass surfaces and the index to set a lens's focal length

The focal length of a thin lens is fixed by the curvature of its two faces and the refractive index of the glass. Steeper curves and higher index mean more bending power.

Set both radii (positive = centre of curvature to the right) and the index. The lens silhouette and the converging parallel rays redraw, and the gold readout gives the focal length.
